tEn: Deep Speech 2: End-to-End Speech Recognition in English and Mandarin
tZh: Deep Speech 2：英语和普通话的端到端语音识别
sEn: End-to-end deep learning achieves competitive speech recognition in English and Mandarin, accelerated by HPC techniques.
sZh: 端到端深度学习在英语和普通话语音识别上达到竞争性水平，并通过高性能计算技术实现加速。
absEn: We show that an end-to-end deep learning approach can be used to recognize either English or Mandarin Chinese speech--two vastly different languages. Because it replaces entire pipelines of hand-engineered components with neural networks, end-to-end learning allows us to handle a diverse variety of speech including noisy environments, accents and different languages. Key to our approach is our application of HPC techniques, resulting in a 7x speedup over our previous system. Because of this efficiency, experiments that previously took weeks now run in days. This enables us to iterate more quickly to identify superior architectures and algorithms. As a result, in several cases, our system is competitive with the transcription of human workers when benchmarked on standard datasets. Finally, using a technique called Batch Dispatch with GPUs in the data center, we show that our system can be inexpensively deployed in an online setting, delivering low latency when serving users at scale.
absZh: 我们展示了一种端到端的深度学习方法，可用于识别英语和普通话这两种截然不同的语言。由于用神经网络取代了整个人工设计的组件流水线，端到端学习使我们能够处理多样化的语音，包括嘈杂环境、口音和不同语言。我们方法的关键是应用高性能计算技术，相比之前的系统实现了7倍的加速。由于这种效率，以前需要数周的实验现在只需几天即可完成。这使我们能够更快地迭代，以识别更优的架构和算法。因此，在多个案例中，我们的系统在标准数据集上的表现与人工转录员相当。最后，通过使用数据中心GPU的批量调度技术，我们展示了系统可以在线低成本部署，在服务大规模用户时提供低延迟。

contrib.en: End-to-end deep learning replaces hand-engineered ASR pipelines with neural networks, handling diverse speech.
contrib.zh: 端到端深度学习用神经网络取代手工设计的ASR流水线，处理多样化语音。

contrib.en: HPC techniques achieve 7x speedup over prior system, reducing training from weeks to days.
contrib.zh: HPC技术实现相比先前系统7倍加速，将训练从数周缩短至数天。

contrib.en: Batch Normalization for RNNs improves convergence and generalization in deep networks.
contrib.zh: RNN的批归一化改善深层网络的收敛和泛化。

contrib.en: SortaGrad curriculum learning stabilizes training by ordering examples by length.
contrib.zh: SortaGrad课程学习通过按长度排序样本稳定训练。

contrib.en: System matches or exceeds human transcription accuracy on several benchmarks.
contrib.zh: 系统在多个基准上匹配或超越人类转录准确率。

contrib.en: Batch Dispatch with GPUs enables low-latency online deployment at scale.
contrib.zh: GPU批量调度实现大规模低延迟在线部署。

limits.en: System requires large labeled datasets (11,940 hours English, 9,400 hours Mandarin).
limits.zh: 系统需要大量标注数据（英语11,940小时，普通话9,400小时）。

limits.en: Bidirectional RNNs hinder low-latency streaming; unidirectional alternatives may lose accuracy.
limits.zh: 双向RNN阻碍低延迟流式处理；单向替代可能损失准确率。

limits.en: Performance on noisy real-world data still lags behind human level.
limits.zh: 在真实噪声数据上的性能仍落后于人类水平。

limits.en: Language model integration requires external text corpora and tuning.
limits.zh: 语言模型集成需要外部文本语料和调参。

limits.en: GPU memory constraints limit model depth and batch size for long utterances.
limits.zh: GPU内存限制长话语的模型深度和批大小。

items.en: We show that an end-to-end deep learning approach can be used to recognize either English or Mandarin Chinese speech—two vastly different languages. Because it replaces entire pipelines of hand-engineered components with neural networks, end-to-end learning allows us to handle a diverse variety of speech including noisy environments, accents and different languages. Key to our approach is our application of HPC techniques, resulting in a 7x speedup over our previous system [26]. Because of this efficiency, experiments that previously took weeks now run in days. This enables us to iterate more quickly to identify superior architectures and algorithms. As a result, in several cases, our system is competitive with the transcription of human workers when benchmarked on standard datasets. Finally, using a technique called Batch Dispatch with GPUs in the data center, we show that our system can be inexpensively deployed in an online setting, delivering low latency when serving users at scale.
items.zh: 我们证明，端到端深度学习方法可用于识别英语或普通话这两种截然不同的语言。由于它用神经网络取代了整个人工设计的组件流水线，端到端学习使我们能够处理多种多样的语音，包括嘈杂环境、口音和不同语言。我们方法的关键在于应用了高性能计算（HPC）技术，相比之前的系统[26]实现了7倍加速。由于这种效率，以前需要数周的实验现在只需几天即可完成。这使我们能够更快地迭代，以识别更优的架构和算法。因此，在几种情况下，我们的系统在标准数据集上的基准测试中与人类转录员的水平相当。最后，通过一种称为批量调度（Batch Dispatch）的技术，在数据中心使用GPU，我们展示了系统可以低成本地在线部署，在服务大规模用户时提供低延迟。

items.en: Decades worth of hand-engineered domain knowledge has gone into current state-of-the-art automatic speech recognition (ASR) pipelines. A simple but powerful alternative solution is to train such ASR models end-to-end, using deep learning to replace most modules with a single model [26]. We present the second generation of our speech system that exemplifies the major advantages of end-to-end learning. The Deep Speech 2 ASR pipeline approaches or exceeds the accuracy of Amazon Mechanical Turk human workers on several benchmarks, works in multiple languages with little modification, and is deployable in a production setting. It thus represents a significant step towards a single ASR system that addresses the entire range of speech recognition contexts handled by humans. Since our system is built on end-to-end deep learning, we can employ a spectrum of deep learning techniques: capturing large training sets, training larger models with high performance computing, and methodically exploring the space of neural network architectures. We show that through these techniques we are able to reduce error rates of our previous end-to-end system [26] in English by up to 43%, and can also recognize Mandarin speech with high accuracy.
items.zh: 当前最先进的自动语音识别（ASR）流水线中融入了数十年的手工领域知识。一个简单但强大的替代方案是端到端训练此类ASR模型，使用深度学习将大多数模块替换为单一模型[26]。我们展示了第二代语音系统，它体现了端到端学习的主要优势。Deep Speech 2 ASR流水线在多个基准测试中接近或超过了Amazon Mechanical Turk人类转录员的准确率，几乎无需修改即可处理多种语言，并且可部署到生产环境中。因此，它代表了朝着单一ASR系统迈出的重要一步，该系统能够处理人类所应对的全部语音识别场景。由于我们的系统基于端到端深度学习，我们可以采用一系列深度学习技术：获取大规模训练集、使用高性能计算训练更大模型，以及系统性地探索神经网络架构空间。我们证明，通过这些技术，我们能够将之前端到端系统[26]在英语中的错误率降低高达43%，并且也能以高准确率识别普通话语音。

items.en: One of the challenges of speech recognition is the wide range of variability in speech and acoustics. As a result, modern ASR pipelines are made up of numerous components including complex feature extraction, acoustic models, language and pronunciation models, speaker adaptation, etc. Building and tuning these individual components makes developing a new speech recognizer very hard, especially for a new language. Indeed, many parts do not generalize well across environments or languages and it is often necessary to support multiple application-specific systems in order to provide acceptable accuracy. This state of affairs is different from human speech recognition: people have the innate ability to learn any language during childhood, using general skills to learn language. After learning to read and write, most humans can transcribe speech with robustness to variation in environment, speaker accent and noise, without additional training for the transcription task. To meet the expectations of speech recognition users, we believe that a single engine must learn to be similarly competent; able to handle most applications with only minor modifications and able to learn new languages from scratch without dramatic changes. Our end-to-end system puts this goal within reach, allowing us to approach or exceed the performance of human workers on several tests in two very different languages: Mandarin and English.
items.zh: 语音识别的挑战之一是语音和声学特征的广泛变异性。因此，现代ASR流水线由众多组件组成，包括复杂的特征提取、声学模型、语言和发音模型、说话人自适应等。构建和调优这些独立组件使得开发新的语音识别器非常困难，尤其是针对新语言。事实上，许多组件在不同环境或语言中泛化能力不佳，通常需要支持多个特定应用的系统才能提供可接受的准确率。这种状况与人类语音识别不同：人类天生具有在童年学习任何语言的能力，使用通用技能来学习语言。在学会读写后，大多数人类能够鲁棒地转录语音，适应环境、说话人口音和噪声的变化，而无需为转录任务进行额外训练。为了满足语音识别用户的期望，我们相信单一引擎必须学会具备类似的能力；能够处理大多数应用，只需少量修改，并且能够从头学习新语言而无需剧烈变化。我们的端到端系统使这一目标触手可及，让我们在两种截然不同的语言——普通话和英语的多个测试中接近或超过人类工人的表现。

items.en: Since Deep Speech 2 (DS2) is an end-to-end deep learning system, we can achieve performance gains by focusing on three crucial components: the model architecture, large labeled training datasets, and computational scale. This approach has also yielded great advances in other application areas such as computer vision and natural language. This paper details our contribution to these three areas for speech recognition, including an extensive investigation of model architectures and the effect of data and model size on recognition performance. In particular, we describe numerous experiments with neural networks trained with the Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) loss function [22] to predict speech transcriptions from audio. We consider networks composed of many layers of recurrent connections, convolutional filters, and nonlinearities, as well as the impact of a specific instance of Batch Normalization [63] (BatchNorm) applied to RNNs. We not only find networks that produce much better predictions than those in previous work [26], but also find instances of recurrent models that can be deployed in a production setting with no significant loss in accuracy.
items.zh: 由于Deep Speech 2（DS2）是一个端到端深度学习系统，我们可以通过关注三个关键组件来实现性能提升：模型架构、大规模标注训练数据集和计算规模。这种方法也在其他应用领域（如计算机视觉和自然语言处理）取得了巨大进展。本文详细介绍了我们在语音识别这三个方面的贡献，包括对模型架构的广泛研究，以及数据和模型大小对识别性能的影响。特别是，我们描述了使用连接主义时间分类（CTC）损失函数[22]训练的神经网络的大量实验，以从音频预测语音转录。我们考虑了由多层循环连接、卷积滤波器和非线性组成的网络，以及应用于RNN的批量归一化（BatchNorm）[63]特定实例的影响。我们不仅找到了比先前工作[26]产生更好预测的网络，还发现了可以在生产环境中部署且准确率无明显损失的循环模型实例。

items.en: Beyond the search for better model architecture, deep learning systems benefit greatly from large quantities of training data. We detail our data capturing pipeline that has enabled us to create larger datasets than what is typically used to train speech recognition systems. Our English speech system is trained on 11,940 hours of speech, while the Mandarin system is trained on 9,400 hours. We use data synthesis to further augment the data during training.
items.zh: 除了寻找更好的模型架构，深度学习系统还从大量训练数据中受益匪浅。我们详细介绍了数据采集流水线，该流水线使我们能够创建比通常用于训练语音识别系统的数据集更大的数据集。我们的英语语音系统在11,940小时的语音上训练，而普通话系统在9,400小时上训练。我们使用数据合成在训练期间进一步扩充数据。

items.en: Training on large quantities of data usually requires the use of larger models. Indeed, our models have many more parameters than those used in our previous system. Training a single model at these scales requires tens of exaFLOPs1111 exaFLOP = 1018superscript101810^{18} FLoating-point OPerations. that would require 3-6 weeks to execute on a single GPU. This makes model exploration a very time consuming exercise, so we have built a highly optimized training system that uses 8 or 16 GPUs to train one model. In contrast to previous large-scale training approaches that use parameter servers and asynchronous updates [18, 10], we use synchronous SGD, which is easier to debug while testing new ideas, and also converges faster for the same degree of data parallelism. To make the entire system efficient, we describe optimizations for a single GPU as well as improvements to scalability for multiple GPUs. We employ optimization techniques typically found in High Performance Computing to improve scalability. These optimizations include a fast implementation of the CTC loss function on the GPU, and a custom memory allocator. We also use carefully integrated compute nodes and a custom implementation of all-reduce to accelerate inter-GPU communication. Overall the system sustains approximately 50 teraFLOP/second when training on 16 GPUs. This amounts to 3 teraFLOP/second per GPU which is about 50% of peak theoretical performance. This scalability and efficiency cuts training times down to 3 to 5 days, allowing us to iterate more quickly on our models and datasets.
items.zh: 在大量数据上训练通常需要使用更大的模型。事实上，我们的模型比之前系统使用的模型拥有更多参数。在这种规模下训练单个模型需要数十exaFLOPs（1 exaFLOP = 10^18次浮点运算），在单个GPU上执行需要3-6周。这使得模型探索非常耗时，因此我们构建了一个高度优化的训练系统，使用8或16个GPU训练一个模型。与之前使用参数服务器和异步更新的大规模训练方法[18, 10]不同，我们使用同步SGD，这在测试新想法时更容易调试，并且在相同的数据并行度下收敛更快。为了使整个系统高效，我们描述了单个GPU的优化以及多GPU可扩展性的改进。我们采用了高性能计算中常见的优化技术来提高可扩展性。这些优化包括在GPU上快速实现CTC损失函数，以及自定义内存分配器。我们还使用精心集成的计算节点和自定义的全规约实现来加速GPU间通信。总体而言，系统在16个GPU上训练时可持续约50 teraFLOP/秒。这相当于每个GPU 3 teraFLOP/秒，约为理论峰值性能的50%。这种可扩展性和效率将训练时间缩短到3到5天，使我们能够更快地迭代模型和数据集。

items.en: We benchmark our system on several publicly available test sets and compare the results to our previous end-to-end system [26]. Our goal is to eventually reach human-level performance not only on specific benchmarks, where it is possible to improve through dataset-specific tuning, but on a range of benchmarks that reflects a diverse set of scenarios. To that end, we have also measured the performance of human workers on each benchmark for comparison. We find that our system outperforms humans in some commonly-studied benchmarks and has significantly closed the gap in much harder cases. In addition to public benchmarks, we show the performance of our Mandarin system on internal datasets that reflect real-world product scenarios.
items.zh: 我们在几个公开可用的测试集上对系统进行基准测试，并将结果与之前的端到端系统[26]进行比较。我们的目标是最终达到人类水平的性能，不仅是在特定基准测试上（可以通过数据集特定调优来提高），而且是在反映多样化场景的一系列基准测试上。为此，我们还测量了人类工人在每个基准测试上的表现以进行比较。我们发现，我们的系统在一些常用基准测试中优于人类，并在更困难的情况下显著缩小了差距。除了公共基准测试，我们还展示了普通话系统在反映真实产品场景的内部数据集上的性能。

items.en: Deep learning systems can be challenging to deploy at scale. Large neural networks are computationally expensive to evaluate for each user utterance, and some network architectures are more easily deployed than others. Through model exploration, we find high-accuracy, deployable network architectures, which we detail here. We also employ a batching scheme suitable for GPU hardware called Batch Dispatch that leads to an efficient, real-time implementation of our Mandarin engine on production servers. Our implementation achieves a 98th percentile compute latency of 67 milliseconds, while the server is loaded with 10 simultaneous audio streams.
items.zh: 深度学习系统在大规模部署时可能具有挑战性。大型神经网络在评估每个用户话语时计算成本高昂，并且某些网络架构比其他架构更容易部署。通过模型探索，我们找到了高精度、可部署的网络架构，我们在此详细说明。我们还采用了一种适用于GPU硬件的批处理方案，称为批量调度（Batch Dispatch），该方案使得我们的普通话引擎在生产服务器上实现了高效、实时的实现。我们的实现达到了67毫秒的98百分位计算延迟，同时服务器负载了10个并发音频流。

items.en: The remainder of the paper is as follows. We begin with a review of related work in deep learning, end-to-end speech recognition, and scalability in Section 2. Section 3 describes the architectural and algorithmic improvements to the model and Section 4 explains how to efficiently compute them. We discuss the training data and steps taken to further augment the training set in Section 5. An analysis of results for the DS2 system in English and Mandarin is presented in Section 6. We end with a description of the steps needed to deploy DS2 to real users in Section 7.
items.zh: 本文的其余部分如下。我们从第2节回顾深度学习、端到端语音识别和可扩展性方面的相关工作开始。第3节描述了模型架构和算法的改进，第4节解释了如何高效计算它们。我们在第5节讨论训练数据以及进一步扩充训练集的步骤。第6节介绍了DS2系统在英语和普通话中的结果分析。最后，我们在第7节描述了将DS2部署给真实用户所需的步骤。

items.en: This work is inspired by previous work in both deep learning and speech recognition. Feed-forward neural network acoustic models were explored more than 20 years ago [7, 50, 19]. Recurrent neural networks and networks with convolution were also used in speech recognition around the same time [51, 67]. More recently DNNs have become a fixture in the ASR pipeline with almost all state of the art speech work containing some form of deep neural network [42, 29, 17, 16, 43, 58]. Convolutional networks have also been found beneficial for acoustic models [1, 53]. Recurrent neural networks, typically LSTMs, are just beginning to be deployed in state-of-the art recognizers [24, 25, 55] and work well together with convolutional layers for the feature extraction [52]. Models with both bidirectional [24] and unidirectional recurrence have been explored as well.
items.zh: 这项工作受到深度学习和语音识别领域先前工作的启发。前馈神经网络声学模型在20多年前就被探索过[7, 50, 19]。循环神经网络和卷积网络也在同一时期被用于语音识别[51, 67]。最近，DNN已成为ASR流水线中的固定组件，几乎所有最先进的语音工作都包含某种形式的深度神经网络[42, 29, 17, 16, 43, 58]。卷积网络也被发现对声学模型有益[1, 53]。循环神经网络，通常是LSTM，刚刚开始被部署在最先进的识别器中[24, 25, 55]，并且与卷积层结合用于特征提取效果良好[52]。双向[24]和单向循环的模型也已被探索。

items.en: End-to-end speech recognition is an active area of research, showing compelling results when used to re-score the outputs of a DNN-HMM [23] and standalone [26]. Two methods are currently used to map variable length audio sequences directly to variable length transcriptions. The RNN encoder-decoder paradigm uses an encoder RNN to map the input to a fixed length vector and a decoder network to expand the fixed length vector into a sequence of output predictions [11, 62]. Adding an attentional mechanism to the decoder greatly improves performance of the system, particularly with long inputs or outputs [2]. In speech, the RNN encoder-decoder with attention performs well both in predicting phonemes [12] or graphemes [3, 8].
items.zh: 端到端语音识别是一个活跃的研究领域，在用于对DNN-HMM[23]的输出进行重新评分以及独立使用时[26]显示出令人信服的结果。目前有两种方法用于将可变长度音频序列直接映射到可变长度转录。RNN编码器-解码器范式使用编码器RNN将输入映射到固定长度向量，并使用解码器网络将固定长度向量扩展为输出预测序列[11, 62]。在解码器中添加注意力机制大大提高了系统的性能，尤其是在输入或输出较长时[2]。在语音中，带有注意力的RNN编码器-解码器在预测音素[12]或字素[3, 8]方面表现良好。

items.en: The other commonly used technique for mapping variable length audio input to variable length output is the CTC loss function [22] coupled with an RNN to model temporal information. The CTC-RNN model performs well in end-to-end speech recognition with grapheme outputs [23, 27, 26, 40]. The CTC-RNN model has also been shown to work well in predicting phonemes [41, 54], though a lexicon is still needed in this case. Furthermore it has been necessary to pre-train the CTC-RNN network with a DNN cross-entropy network that is fed frame-wise alignments from a GMM-HMM system [54]. In contrast, we train the CTC-RNN networks from scratch without the need of frame-wise alignments for pre-training.
items.zh: 另一种常用的将可变长度音频输入映射到可变长度输出的技术是CTC损失函数[22]结合RNN来建模时间信息。CTC-RNN模型在具有字素输出的端到端语音识别中表现良好[23, 27, 26, 40]。CTC-RNN模型也被证明在预测音素方面效果良好[41, 54]，尽管在这种情况下仍然需要词典。此外，有必要使用来自GMM-HMM系统的帧级对齐来预训练CTC-RNN网络[54]。相比之下，我们从零开始训练CTC-RNN网络，无需帧级对齐进行预训练。

items.en: Exploiting scale in deep learning has been central to the success of the field thus far [36, 38]. Training on a single GPU resulted in substantial performance gains [49], which were subsequently scaled linearly to two [36] or more GPUs [15]. We take advantage of work in increasing individual GPU efficiency for low-level deep learning primitives [9]. We build on the past work in using model-parallelism [15], data-parallelism [18] or a combination of the two [64, 26] to create a fast and highly scalable system for training deep RNNs in speech recognition.
items.zh: 利用深度学习中的规模优势一直是该领域成功的关键[36, 38]。在单个GPU上训练带来了显著的性能提升[49]，随后线性扩展到两个[36]或更多GPU[15]。我们利用了提高单个GPU效率的低级深度学习原语工作[9]。我们基于过去的工作，使用模型并行[15]、数据并行[18]或两者的结合[64, 26]来创建一个快速且高度可扩展的系统，用于训练语音识别中的深度RNN。

items.en: Data has also been central to the success of end-to-end speech recognition, with over 7000 hours of labeled speech used in Deep Speech 1 (DS1) [26]. Data augmentation has been highly effective in improving the performance of deep learning in computer vision [39, 56, 14]. This has also been shown to improve speech systems [21, 26]. Techniques used for data augmentation in speech range from simple noise addition [26] to complex perturbations such as simulating changes to the vocal tract length and rate of speech of the speaker [31, 35].
items.zh: 数据也是端到端语音识别成功的关键，Deep Speech 1（DS1）[26]使用了超过7000小时的标注语音。数据增强在提高计算机视觉深度学习性能方面非常有效[39, 56, 14]。这也被证明可以改善语音系统[21, 26]。语音数据增强使用的技术范围从简单的噪声添加[26]到复杂的扰动，例如模拟声道长度和说话人语速的变化[31, 35]。

items.en: Existing speech systems can also be used to bootstrap new data collection. In one approach, the authors use one speech engine to align and filter a thousand hours of read speech [46]. In another approach, a heavy-weight offline speech recognizer is used to generate transcriptions for tens of thousands of hours of speech [33]. This is then passed through a filter and used to re-train the recognizer, resulting in significant performance gains. We draw inspiration from these past approaches in bootstrapping larger datasets and data augmentation to increase the effective amount of labeled data for our system.
items.zh: 现有的语音系统也可用于引导新的数据收集。在一种方法中，作者使用一个语音引擎来对齐和过滤一千小时的朗读语音[46]。在另一种方法中，使用一个重量级的离线语音识别器为数万小时的语音生成转录[33]。然后通过过滤器，并用于重新训练识别器，从而带来显著的性能提升。我们从这些过去的方法中汲取灵感，用于引导更大的数据集和数据增强，以增加我们系统的有效标注数据量。

items.en: A simple multi-layer model with a single recurrent layer cannot exploit thousands of hours of labelled speech. In order to learn from datasets this large, we increase the model capacity via depth. We explore architectures with up to 11 layers including many bidirectional recurrent layers and convolutional layers. These models have nearly 8 times the amount of computation per data example as the models in Deep Speech 1 making fast optimization and computation critical. In order to optimize these models successfully, we use Batch Normalization for RNNs and a novel optimization curriculum we call SortaGrad. We also exploit long strides between RNN inputs to reduce computation per example by a factor of 3. This is helpful for both training and evaluation, though requires some modifications in order to work well with CTC. Finally, though many of our research results make use of bidirectional recurrent layers, we find that excellent models exist using only unidirectional recurrent layers—a feature that makes such models much easier to deploy. Taken together these features allow us to tractably optimize deep RNNs and improve performance by more than 40% in both English and Mandarin error rates over the smaller baseline models.
items.zh: 一个具有单循环层的简单多层模型无法利用数千小时的标注语音。为了从如此大的数据集中学习，我们通过深度增加模型容量。我们探索了多达11层的架构，包括许多双向循环层和卷积层。这些模型每个数据示例的计算量几乎是Deep Speech 1模型的8倍，这使得快速优化和计算变得至关重要。为了成功优化这些模型，我们使用了针对RNN的批量归一化（Batch Normalization）和一种新颖的优化课程，我们称之为SortaGrad。我们还利用RNN输入之间的长步幅，将每个示例的计算量减少了3倍。这对训练和评估都有帮助，但需要一些修改才能与CTC良好配合。最后，尽管我们的许多研究结果使用了双向循环层，但我们发现仅使用单向循环层也存在优秀的模型——这一特性使得此类模型更容易部署。综合这些特性，我们能够可行地优化深度RNN，并在英语和普通话的错误率上比较小的基线模型提高了40%以上。

items.en: The model is trained using the CTC loss function [22]. Given an input-output pair (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y) and the current parameters of the network θ𝜃\theta, we compute the loss function ℒ​(x,y;θ)ℒ𝑥𝑦𝜃\mathcal{L}(x,y;\theta) and its derivative with respect to the parameters of the network ∇θℒ​(x,y;θ)subscript∇𝜃ℒ𝑥𝑦𝜃\nabla_{\theta}\mathcal{L}(x,y;\theta). This derivative is then used to update the network parameters through the backpropagation through time algorithm.
items.zh: 模型使用 CTC 损失函数 [22] 进行训练。给定输入-输出对 (x,y) 和网络的当前参数 θ，我们计算损失函数 L(x,y;θ) 及其相对于网络参数的梯度 ∇_θ L(x,y;θ)。然后，该梯度通过时间反向传播算法用于更新网络参数。

items.en: In the following subsections we describe the architectural and algorithmic improvements made relative to DS1 [26]. Unless otherwise stated these improvements are language agnostic. We report results on an English speaker held out development set which is an internal dataset containing 2048 utterances of primarily read speech. All models are trained on datasets described in Section 5. We report Word Error Rate (WER) for the English system and Character Error Rate (CER) for the Mandarin system. In both cases we integrate a language model in a beam search decoding step as described in Section 3.8.
items.zh: 在以下小节中，我们描述了相对于 DS1 [26] 的架构和算法改进。除非另有说明，这些改进与语言无关。我们在一个英语说话者保留开发集上报告结果，该开发集是一个内部数据集，包含 2048 段以朗读语音为主的语音。所有模型均在第五节描述的数据集上训练。我们报告英语系统的词错误率（WER）和普通话系统的字符错误率（CER）。在这两种情况下，我们都在束搜索解码步骤中集成语言模型，如第 3.8 节所述。

items.en: To efficiently scale our model as we scale the training set, we increase the depth of the networks by adding more hidden layers, rather than making each layer larger. Previous work has examined doing so by increasing the number of consecutive bidirectional recurrent layers [24]. We explore Batch Normalization (BatchNorm) as a technique to accelerate training for such networks [63] since they often suffer from optimization issues.
items.zh: 为了在扩展训练集时高效地扩展模型，我们通过增加更多隐藏层来增加网络深度，而不是使每层更大。先前的工作研究了通过增加连续双向循环层的数量来实现这一点 [24]。我们探索了批归一化（BatchNorm）作为加速此类网络训练的技术 [63]，因为它们经常面临优化问题。

items.en: Recent research has shown that BatchNorm improves the speed of convergence of recurrent nets, without showing any improvement in generalization performance [37]. In contrast, we demonstrate that when applied to very deep networks of simple RNNs on large data sets, batch normalization substantially improves final generalization error while greatly accelerating training.
items.zh: 最近的研究表明，BatchNorm 提高了循环网络的收敛速度，但没有显示出泛化性能的改善 [37]。相比之下，我们证明，当应用于大数据集上的简单 RNN 深度网络时，批归一化显著改善了最终泛化误差，同时大大加速了训练。

items.en: In a typical feed-forward layer containing an affine transformation followed by a non-linearity f​(⋅)𝑓⋅f(\cdot), we insert a BatchNorm transformation by applying f​(ℬ​(W​h))𝑓ℬ𝑊ℎf(\mathcal{B}(Wh)) instead of f​(W​h+b)𝑓𝑊ℎ𝑏f(Wh+b), where

items.en: The terms EE\mathrm{E} and VarVar\mathrm{Var} are the empirical mean and variance over a minibatch. The bias b𝑏b of the layer is dropped since its effect is cancelled by mean removal. The learnable parameters γ𝛾\gamma and β𝛽\beta allow the layer to scale and shift each hidden unit as desired. The constant ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon is small and positive, and is included only for numerical stability. In our convolutional layers the mean and variance are estimated over all the temporal output units for a given convolutional filter on a minibatch. The BatchNorm transformation reduces internal covariate shift by insulating a given layer from potentially uninteresting changes in the mean and variance of the layer’s input.
items.zh: 项 E 和 Var 是小批量上的经验均值和方差。该层的偏置 b 被丢弃，因为其效果被均值移除抵消。可学习参数 γ 和 β 允许该层根据需要缩放和移动每个隐藏单元。常数 ε 很小且为正，仅用于数值稳定性。在我们的卷积层中，均值和方差是在小批量上针对给定卷积滤波器的所有时间输出单元估计的。BatchNorm 变换通过使给定层免受其输入均值和方差中潜在无意义变化的影响，减少了内部协变量偏移。

items.en: In this case the mean and variance statistics are accumulated over a single time-step of the minibatch. The sequential dependence between time-steps prevents averaging over all time-steps. We find that this technique does not lead to improvements in optimization. We also tried accumulating an average over successive time-steps, so later time-steps are normalized over all present and previous time-steps. This also proved ineffective and greatly complicated backpropagation.
items.zh: 在这种情况下，均值和方差统计量是在小批量的单个时间步上累积的。时间步之间的顺序依赖性阻止了对所有时间步进行平均。我们发现这种技术不会带来优化改进。我们还尝试累积连续时间步的平均值，以便后面的时间步根据所有当前和先前的时间步进行归一化。这也被证明是无效的，并且大大复杂化了反向传播。

items.en: For each hidden unit, we compute the mean and variance statistics over all items in the minibatch over the length of the sequence. Figure 2 shows that deep networks converge faster with sequence-wise normalization. Table 1 shows that the performance improvement from sequence-wise normalization increases with the depth of the network, with a 12% performance difference for the deepest network. When comparing depth, in order to control for model size we hold constant the total number of parameters and still see strong performance gains. We would expect to see even larger improvements from depth if we held constant the number of activations per layer and added layers. We also find that BatchNorm harms generalization error for the shallowest network just as it converges slower for shallower networks.
items.zh: 对于每个隐藏单元，我们计算小批量中所有项在序列长度上的均值和方差统计量。图 2 显示，深度网络通过序列级归一化收敛更快。表 1 显示，序列级归一化的性能改进随着网络深度的增加而增加，对于最深的网络，性能差异为 12%。在比较深度时，为了控制模型大小，我们保持总参数数量不变，仍然看到了显著的性能提升。如果我们保持每层激活数量不变并增加层数，我们预计会看到深度带来的更大改进。我们还发现，BatchNorm 对最浅网络的泛化误差有害，就像它对浅网络收敛更慢一样。

items.en: The BatchNorm approach works well in training, but is difficult to implement for a deployed ASR system, since it is often necessary to evaluate a single utterance in deployment rather than a batch. We find that normalizing each neuron to its mean and variance over just the sequence degrades performance. Instead, we store a running average of the mean and variance for the neuron collected during training, and use these for evaluation in deployment [63]. Using this technique, we can evaluate a single utterance at a time with better results than evaluating with a large batch.
items.zh: BatchNorm 方法在训练中效果良好，但难以在部署的 ASR 系统中实现，因为在部署中通常需要评估单个话语而不是一个批次。我们发现，仅对序列上的每个神经元进行均值和方差归一化会降低性能。相反，我们存储训练期间收集的神经元均值和方差的运行平均值，并在部署评估中使用它们 [63]。使用这种技术，我们可以一次评估单个话语，结果比使用大批次评估更好。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Training curves of two models trained with and without BatchNorm. We start the plot after the first epoch of training as the curve is more difficult to interpret due to the SortaGrad curriculum method mentioned in Section 3.3
items.capZh: 图 2：使用和不使用 BatchNorm 训练的两个模型的训练曲线。我们在训练第一个 epoch 后开始绘图，因为由于第 3.3 节中提到的 SortaGrad 课程方法，曲线更难以解释。

items.en: Training on examples of varying length pose some algorithmic challenges. One possible solution is truncating backpropagation through time [68], so that all examples have the same sequence length during training [52]. However, this can inhibit the ability to learn longer term dependencies. Other works have found that presenting examples in order of difficulty can accelerate online learning [6, 70]. A common theme in many sequence learning problems including machine translation and speech recognition is that longer examples tend to be more challenging [11].
items.zh: 对长度不同的样本进行训练带来了一些算法挑战。一种可能的解决方案是截断时间反向传播 [68]，以便所有样本在训练期间具有相同的序列长度 [52]。然而，这可能会抑制学习长期依赖关系的能力。其他工作发现，按难度顺序呈现样本可以加速在线学习 [6, 70]。在许多序列学习问题（包括机器翻译和语音识别）中，一个共同主题是较长的样本往往更具挑战性 [11]。
